description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a rich, dark brown wood grain finish. The front panel is adorned with a beige speaker grille occupying the upper half, bordered by a metallic or plastic trim. Below the grille, there is a horizontal tuning scale with frequency markings, flanked by two large round knobs likely for tuning and volume control. Several rectangular push buttons are situated beneath the tuning scale, suggesting preset or function selection. The back of the radio has visible power cord and plug, along with small cutouts for ventilation and possibly external connections. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a beige speaker grille, tuning scale, two large knobs, and several push buttons on the front. The back features a power cord and ventilation cutouts.
description_view_0: The left side of the radio shows a plain wooden surface with visible wood grain and a portion of the power cord emerging from the back.
description_view_1: The rear view displays the flat wooden back panel, power cord, plug, and small cutouts for ventilation or connections.
description_view_2: The right side is a plain wooden surface similar to the left, with the power cord trailing out of view.
description_view_3: The front view highlights the beige speaker grille, tuning scale, two large knobs, and several rectangular push buttons, making it the interactive face of the radio.
